title: BLKANDBLK error on SV queue
body:
Version: Verilator 5.012 2023-06-13 rev v5.012-1-g9fb17e532

Hi, I'm trying to reuse some DPI code that makes use of SV queues but am seeing an error reported.  Initially I realised that this probably isn't supported as it's not synthesisable but I found at least one other ticket with someone using a queue so thought I'd check.

```
%Error-BLKANDNBLK: src/idex_vip_swd_if_dpi_wrapper_0/src/rtl/swd_if_dpi_wrapper.sv:53:16: Unsupported: Blocked and non-blocking assignments to same variable: 'u_swd_if_dpi.swj_seq_data_queue'
                                                                                        : ... In instance chip_sim_tb
   53 |   bit [31:0]   swj_seq_data_queue  [$];
      |                ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                   src/idex_vip_swd_if_dpi_wrapper_0/src/rtl/swd_if_dpi_wrapper.sv:105:17: ... Location of blocking assignment
  105 |   function void grpc_server_swj_sequence_push( input bit [31:0] value );
      |                 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                   src/idex_vip_swd_if_dpi_wrapper_0/src/rtl/swd_if_dpi_wrapper.sv:163:30: ... Location of nonblocking assignment
  163 |               swj_seq_data_r <= swj_seq_data_queue.pop_front();
      |                              ^~
%Error: Internal Error: src/idex_vip_swd_if_dpi_wrapper_0/src/rtl/swd_if_dpi_wrapper.sv:163:33: ../V3Delayed.cpp:564: <= on read+write method
                                                                                              : ... In instance chip_sim_tb
  163 |               swj_seq_data_r <= swj_seq_data_queue.pop_front();
      |                                 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

The SV itself is pretty simple.  I declare the queue, it gets pushed by a DPI call and then popped in an always block.

```systemverilog

  bit [31:0]   swj_seq_data_queue  [$];

  function void grpc_server_swj_sequence_push( input bit [31:0] value );
    swj_seq_data_queue.push_back(value);
  endfunction

  always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin : swj_seq_write_data_fifo
    if (!rst_n) begin
      swj_seq_data_r       <= '0;
      swj_seq_data_valid_r <= '0;
    end else begin
      case(swj_seq_data_valid_r)
        1'b0 : begin
          if (swj_seq_data_queue.size() > 0) begin
            swj_seq_data_r       <= swj_seq_data_queue.pop_front();
            swj_seq_data_valid_r <= '1;
          end
        end
        <snip>
```

Any reason I can't do this?
